The Blind Eye (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 2
Speaker 1: Hey guys, and welcome back. This is theme plate 14, The Blind Eye. And it's gonna be
pretty similar to the point of no return theme plate that we just explored previously,
except this one is used to really fully release your prospect from those past failures and
remind them of their responsibility to act on the better alternative now available to
them. So it has this dynamic of coddling, and then challenging and confronting. So it acts
as that risk revealer and then putting them in a privileged place, a place of exclusive
empowerment to do something about it.
So, here is how it breaks down. We begin by detailing the struggle, that moment of
highest tension. And then we release them from blame, being very specific as to why it
wasn't their fault. And then we will remind them of those non-serving beliefs you set up
during the re-launch, hold them accountable to the new version. You've given them a
new way, you've shown them a new path, you've flipped their paradigm, you've given
them those aha's and insights, and now you get the right to close that loop. It's not your
fault until it is. That's where you kind of hold their feet to the fire. Make them
accountable for acting on what's now in front of them.
Then you future pace the new outcomes and how your program facilitates it, remind
them once again of the commitments that they made earlier, and close that consistency
loop. So, you opened up those consistency loop in the confirmation, and then the pre-
launch, and now you're closing it. You're using that not your fault until it is dynamic.
So, here is how it plays out. Real talk and a VIP invitation. Almost every other day I get a
teary-eyed Facebook message from a yoga teacher kicking herself for spending years
barely scraping by, doing everything in her power to make it a go despite the silent
doubts from friends and family, hustling around town to teach classes at any studio that
will have her, scrolling through the glossy Instagram quote cards from her favorite yoga
crushes, wondering what the heck must be wrong with her. Why, after all these years,
am I still stuck riding the bus to get to a gig I don't even want to teach?
So, this is that struggle in the rear-view, all that moment of highest tension stuff that
we're bringing back to the surface and getting them intimate with. Ouch. Let's stop the
self-blame for a sec, 'cause here's the thing. It wasn't your fault that your YTT didn't
prepare you for the business world, that it had you believing there was only one
revenue stream available to you, and it required negotiating with stingy studio owners
to get a piece of an ever-shrinking pie, or that it led you to the conclusion that all it
takes is a few crystals, some mantras, and the right sequencing between them to
magically land a slew of high-paying private clients that allow you to make teaching a
full-time thing.
Nope, can't be blamed for just playing out the script you were handed. So here's where
we are releasing them from all that blame. But more importantly, qualifying why,
explaining why it wasn't their fault in a believable and a sincere way. But if the current
definition of being a yoga teacher isn't serving you and hasn't been for a while, you are
responsible for graduating into a new definition. So this is where the accountability
comes into play, one that empowers, one that enriches, one that takes your lofty

intentions and grounds them in the fertile soil of proven online marketing strategies
that have fueled the rapid rise of all your favorite yoga crushes.
You are responsible for learning about the nine revenue streams available to you, four
of which are fully automated and passive. So, here we're actually bringing elements of
the program. We're linking to how ... linking your program as a thing that facilitates this
new paradigm they are responsible for stepping into. So there's that seamless
integration, that re-contextualization, between the beliefs and paradigm they've shifted
into and your program as being the only logical thing that provides it.
And you are responsible for choosing the one that syncs up best with your authentic
expression. We'll help with that in Module 3, and making use of the six group coaching
calls to keep you on track, as for the first time since you began this yoga journey, you
truly feel a sense of, "Wow, I got this." In short, you're responsible for your own
awesomeness, so, holding them accountable again. This email is really high on holding
your prospect to that standard, to that standard of being responsible for their own
outcomes and how you share it with others in a sustainable way that serves all.
Over the last few days, you've steeped in this new definition, you've sketched out some
very real projections for your business, and met the new version of you capable of
carrying them out. Here's your invitation to flow forward and leave the hustle behind.
So, that is it, that call to action that holds them accountable. Once again, this is a pretty
similar email to the one above, you know, the point of no return, except this one has
that dynamic of coddling them from it's not your fault until it is. It's not your fault. You
couldn't have known better. This is just what you were handed. But now that you've
integrated these beliefs, now that you've seen a better way, you absolutely are
responsible for leveraging them and acting upon them. Our program, the thing we offer,
helps you capitalize on that new paradigm, and that's the only one that fits that new
reality that you've been initiated into.
So, that is the full dynamic of the blind eye theme plate. So, this is one of my favorites as
well. It's usually a pretty short one to write. So yeah, give it a shot. Take about 20
minutes, do a sand timer thing if you will, put on some binaural beats, drink some
coffee, do whatever it is you do to get into your zone and see if in about 20 to 30
minutes you can walk away with the first draft of an effective high-converting blind eye
email.
So, that is it. That is it for the sales sequence. I'm losing my voice, so I'm gonna check
out and meet you back when you're ready in Module 7, where we're gonna finally,
finally tackle the close. I'll see you there in a bit.
